signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: RBA raised the cash rate 25bps to 4.35% in an 8-1 vote, creating a -60bp carry deficit for USD. AUD COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) while retail sentiment is 86% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence at 0.7186 suggests retail is fading the hawkish shift; a sustained break higher triggers a retail short-squeeze. Westpac flags further upside inflation risks, supporting the AUD carry advantage.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) shows speculators are heavily fading the RBNZ's hawkish bias. With the RBNZ signaling a hike bias for the 27 May meeting and citing financial system resilience despite Middle East tensions, the pair faces significant long-unwind risk for shorts if the hike is delivered. NZD/USD at 0.5889 remains supported by the 2.25% rate floor despite the 150bp USD carry advantage.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.89 is supported by a 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage, but rising Middle East tensions and Rubio's warnings on Iranian missile capabilities introduce intervention risk as price tests the 160.00 threshold. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) reflects a crowded carry trade. Carry remains the dominant driver while VIX stays at 17.4 (normal volatility), but geopolitical escalation favors JPY safe-haven bids.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Yield Spread
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.709 vs -0.154 norm (z=+2.01σ) indicates a significant regime break where EUR is tracking equity strength rather than traditional yield differentials. Despite a 175bp carry advantage for USD (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%), EUR/USD at 1.1697 remains bid alongside the STOXX 50 (+1.84%). Watch for a normalization of the correlation or a break in equity momentum to re-establish USD carry dominance.
